steps.title: Use the semicircles
steps.content:
The circumference is $144\pi$, so each semicircle has arc length $72\pi$. Since $PR$ and $QS$ are diameters,

$$PQ+QR=72\pi$$

and

$$QR+RS=72\pi$$

This means $PQ=RS$.

steps.title: Use the arc relationship
steps.content:
Let the length of arc $PQ$ be $x$. Then arc $PS$ has length $72\pi-x$. The problem says arc $PS$ is twice arc $PQ$.

$$72\pi-x=2x$$

$$72\pi=3x$$

$$24\pi=x$$

steps.title: Find the requested arc
steps.content: Use $PQ+QR=72\pi$ to find arc $QR$. $$QR=72\pi-24\pi$$ $$QR=48\pi$$ The answer is B.
